blocks.content: What is God?
Operator's Meeting Room

blocks.speaker: The Black Circle Mark
blocks.content: It's over.
The 'Dr. Martin's Counterattack' episode had concluded with an unprecedented victory for the users.

blocks.speaker: The Black Circle Mark
blocks.content: It was a decent episode, considering your incompetence.
The screen for Number Seven, which had been disconnected until now, suddenly lit up.
It was on Wuorin's directive.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Tch! So, what now? Do whatever you want. Revoke my operator rights or something.

blocks.speaker: The Smile Mark
blocks.content: Is it so easy for you to say that? Because of you, High Gear almost got shut down.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: I know! That's why I'm asking—what do you want me to do? I'll transfer all my shares in Under Coder. Satisfied?

blocks.speaker: The Lollipop Mark
blocks.content: Apologizing might be a good start.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: You think I don't want to? I just want to take responsibility. My career's already ruined, and if I leave here, I've got a metaphorical blade at my throat. Just decide how to punish me. Use your creativity. Mess me up as much as you want. I don't ever want to think about High Gear again.
Time couldn't be reversed, and punishing someone in High Gear had its limitations.

blocks.speaker: The Gear Mark
blocks.content: But that's the charm of Under Coder. We're all drawn here by that sense of freedom.

blocks.speaker: The Yo Mark
blocks.content: Fine. Just get lost. What are you even doing here? Anyway, it's always us sane ones cleaning up after people like you. Go ahead, live your pathetic life and die like that.
Number Seven burned with frustration but gritted his teeth, knowing he had to answer for his actions.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: I said I'd take responsibility.

blocks.speaker: The Smile Mark
blocks.content: So how will you take responsibility? Asking us what to do won't help! You should never have made that stupid mistake in the first place! You thought you were something special just because you got a title?

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Ugh, seriously!

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: You can insult me all you want, but let's get one thing straight! You think this is because I had a title? I didn't have a choice!

blocks.speaker: The Smile Mark
blocks.content: What gives you the right to raise your voice?

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Why wouldn't I? Who here worked harder than me? High Gear's creatures? I handled everything! I ran the physics engine tens of thousands of times based on those ridiculous proposals the Operator gave me!
If he could turn back time, he'd wish he'd never met the Operator.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: But what did she do? She flirted with some weirdo, and as if that wasn't enough...
His words trailed off as the vacant screen flickered, and a system message appeared.
-Operator has entered the room.-
The mark symbolizing High Gear blinked, and Number Seven felt his chest tighten.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Number Seven.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: What, what?

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Apologize.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: ...Sorry.
As soon as the words left his mouth, something he had desperately tried to protect crumbled inside him.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I got angry seeing you with that Yahweh guy and wanted to destroy everything. That's the kind of person I am. You know me. I'm petty, selfish, trash.
The room was silent.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: This isn't something you can resolve with an apology. Do you realize how much you hurt me?

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: I know. So tell me—what should I do? I'll do whatever you say. Honestly, I don't know what else to do.
After a moment of thought, she replied,

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Let's meet.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Outside. All the operators. Let's call it our first official meeting. I also have some things to say.

blocks.speaker: The Lollipop Mark
blocks.content: Wow! That sounds fun!
Everyone else stayed silent, but their silence was filled with apprehension.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Uh… that's probably not a good idea.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Anyone who can't come doesn't have to. Some of us aren't human, and not all operators can leave. But Number Seven—you must show up. That's my condition.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: I-I don't want to.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Are you sure? I heard you'll die if you don't get unanimous approval to stay out.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: I'd rather die! How could I face you? No, I just don't want to.

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: Is that so? Then this meeting is over. I'll report that we couldn't forgive you.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Hey! Just this once, let it go. The people out there are terrifying. I'll do anything else you want, okay? Please?

blocks.speaker: The Operator
blocks.content: You have three seconds.

blocks.speaker: Number Seven
blocks.content: Ah…
At Gold Wheel Headquarters, Shirone woke up in the recovery room.
A white ceiling greeted him.
As he slowly sat up, he noticed over 200 beds, all empty.
Fermi was nowhere to be seen.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even Marsha's not here.
The texture of the air he breathed reminded him of where he was.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm back.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Though it might have only been a day, he had experienced so much in High Gear.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yet… it all felt like a fleeting dream.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: An illusion.
As Shirone reflected on what had happened in the outer world, the door opened, and Fermi entered.
Shirone frowned.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We took the same dose, so why is he up before me? He's so irritating.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: You look priceless. Maybe you should pull the blanket up. Don't worry, I'm not going to eat you. Come on, we've got things to settle.
Sticking his tongue out at Fermi's back, Shirone got out of bed and adjusted his senses.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where's Marsha?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: She woke up four hours ago. They all went drinking. They're not coming back tonight.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Is that okay? Drinking right away?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: People live to give their brains pleasure. Sure, some need to save humanity to feel satisfied, but others just live for a good drink.
There was a sharp edge to his words, but Shirone ignored it.
When they reached the top floor of the Golden Wheel building, where Shirone had been a few times before, Fermi gestured to a seat.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Sit down.
Shirone knew what 'settling' meant.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You've made deals with Wuorin, too, haven't you?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I'm just receiving my share of the information. I think I'm free to use it however I want.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who said otherwise? I was just asking if you made a deal with Uorin. No need to get defensive.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I'm just asking if you made a deal with Uorin. No need to get defensive.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We just don't get along.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: There was a reason they had clashed since their school days.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He's straightforward and smart. He knows what information can shake me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It might sound funny to say it was payback for teasing him in the recovery room, but he was definitely that kind of guy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Anyway, it seems like he's going to settle things properly. Well, that's why we make deals.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: It's not the same ratio. I need to think about what the Empress can do for me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hmm.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Fermi and Uorin were individuals with immense influence on the world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Pony has to deal with such people in the Holy War. Of course, there's Mr. Lupist, but…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: From Shirone's perspective, who fought for humanity, all skilled individuals were potential trading partners.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: He went out into the outer world.
Since it was a story often told in the world of the Void, the explanation proceeded quickly.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: ...So that's how it is.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: That's shocking.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: The fact that he showed emotion meant his mind was still full of thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: So, in the end, it's a battle between two universes. The universe of gods and the universe of humans.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's not a battle. It's a complete takeover.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Indeed, it wasn't a battle from the start. Their will acts as a result for us. Also, to maintain that status, this side's universe becomes a multiverse.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's a reasonable view. In short, all universes are signals sent from the outer world. An infinite number of multiverses all digging into the Void.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Because it's the same signal, your senses didn't disappear in the world of High Gear and acted the same.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's right.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: But is it possible? In the outer world of High Gear, you could have been mistaken at any time. That means even your mind was processed as a signal. To compute all that infinite universe, what kind of technology would it take?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The reversal of cause and effect.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Right. Whether it's possible or not doesn't matter. Whatever we dream of, whatever this universe aims to achieve, the final result is…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's already in the minus universe from the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: ...A god?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you going to do now?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: You have to find out. The outer world. You must have felt it in High Gear, but that's the key to Ultima.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Right. My simultaneous event is staying in the Kingdom of Paras. Once the king's approval is given, I'll explore the ancient ruins. They say it's a giant pyramid.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: You're talking about the place where the signal came from in the outer world. Are you sure about this? If your theory is correct and the god is the result, no matter how much you struggle, you won't stand a chance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Not necessarily.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Goffin wanted to become a god with a heart. And he shot Hexa into this universe. What do you think that means?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: ...He shot it from the outer world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Exactly. He broke the perfect law of the gods and sent a single heart. I don't know how, but my existence is proof. That's why I don't give up. If I can return from the outer world and speak of hope…

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Humanity will be united by love.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: For now, we need to focus on what's necessary. The operators of High Gear will gather tomorrow. I'm planning to go. With their help, we'll mine the Apocalypse.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Alright, it's settled. The Empress too, let's not distinguish between allies and enemies for now and dig in. Leave the operator matter to me. I'll make a mining plan with the Parrot Mercenary Corps.
Fermi stood up, pushing his chair.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fermi.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Yes?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why? Do you have more to say?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: About the lost memory. I won't ask about the deal. But shouldn't I know the reason you accepted it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I trust you.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Ah, you want to hear that? I told you I'd tell you from the beginning. Anyway, the decision is yours…

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I trust you.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: I can't judge. So I have to rely on your judgment. If I know the reason I made the deal even at the cost of losing my memory, what happens then?
A silence long enough to feel lengthy passed.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Don't be mistaken. I'm not your friend. Trust me? With that statement, thousands of ways to use you are popping into my head.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And for me?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If I know, I have a chance to prepare. Now that the secret of the outer world is revealed. Considering all that, what do you think? Even now, shouldn't I know?

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: …No.
Before the door closed, a voice was heard.

blocks.speaker: Fermi
blocks.content: Trust me, you fool.
